column2: What is an animal that is distantly related to the capybara and has the densest fur of all land-dwelling mammals?
Who was the United States President when Chile won their first Copa America?
Which female athlete achieved a "world's best" and a "world record"  for the marathon during Tony Blair's second term as British Prime Minister.

Who were the key members of the original lineup of the rock band Queen, and what were their primary roles in the band?  Which original member of Queen was also in a band named Smile and what year did that band begin and end?

In the town where the most successful American Ace of World War 2 to survive that war was born, there is a college. What was the name and percentage of the largest demographic of full and part time students attending that college in 2020?
What geographical commonality do the person who first described Apriona brunneomarginata and painter Wolfgang Hutter share?
What is population in 1968 of the city that hosted the olympic games in which Lucy Steele competed?
There's a comedy club on Sunset in West Hollywood that opened in 1972. One of its founders opened for one of Elvis' comeback performances. Where was this founder's first wife born?
As of August 3, 2024, which band was nominated three times for the Grammy Award for Best Metal Performance and also headlined the Opus Stage at Download Festival 2023?
Spryo the Dragon released a sequel just a year after it came out. In the same year Spryo's sequel came out, what popular skateboarding based game hit shelves?
Who was the president of Kenya when Uduak Amimo took a break from her talk show and the media to pursue her interest in coaching and social impact projects?
Which month had the third-lowest mean daily minimum temperature (recorded from 1876–1905) in the Japanese city which in 1720 was estimated to be the largest in the world?
Which political leader of the countries where the Potsdam agreement and the Wellington Convention were signed was older on the 1st of July 1905?
In the year that the film 'Moana' was released, who was the hitting coach of the team that lost the MLB World Series?
What sea borders the Irish County where the author of the short story Foster was born?
How many times in total did the Argentina women's field hockey team and the Uruguay women's field hockey team enter the Pan American Games and the Olympic Games from 2000 to 2020?
If you add up the birth years of Emma Watson, Daniel Radcliffe and Rupert Grint, what is the sum?

The Eastern Shawnee Tribe of Oklahoma owns and operates the Indigo Sky Casino. Their tribal headquarters are located in a suburb of the metropolitan area of a historic 2011 tornado that killed 158 people. What is the name of the metropolitan area?
How many years apart was the founding of Snell & Wilmer from the birth of the man who got 3rd place at the 1954 Masters golf tournament?
Which other astronaut flew the same type of aircraft during the Korean War as the first man to step on the moon?
How many days did it take for World War 2 to end after the death of Alois Burgstaller?
Adolf Hitler was born exactly 110 years before which US mass shooting?
How old was the 31st President of the United States when the second nuclear weapon ever used in warfare was dropped?
What is the total number of pages in the first two first-edition books in the Emperyan Series by Rebecca Yarros?
As of August 3, 2024, excluding white, what colour features on the flag of the city that houses the racecourse where Danon The Kid made his racing debut?
What was the first skyscraper built in this player's hometown before he became the 160th pick of the 1980's NFL Draft?
Regarding the award that the man who created the initial sketch for the Eiffel Tower received, how many of the award models have hanging devices that are in the shape of a crown?
Excluding blue, what other colour appears on the 2004 flag of the region in which Alastair Galbraith's home city is situated?
When the San Francisco 49ers and San Diego Chargers met in Super Bowl XXIX, what was the #1 film at the box office in the U.S.?
What was the difference in original sale price between Tom Thomson’s “Northern River” and “Northern Lake”
What NBA team was founded three years before the Apollo 11 crew landed on the moon?
How many more career home runs did the MLB player who had the highest slugging percentage in 1954 have than the player who was the the first African American to play in Major League Baseball?
What are the coordinates for the beach outside the UK that shares its name to a Welsh town that is home to the Great Orme?
I'm thinking of the Weird Al Yankovic parody of American Pie, what Weird Al album did it first appear on?
What year was the band leader of the group who originally performed the song sampled in Kayne West's song Power born?
Frank Jevons was the 9th Master of Hatfield College. His death came how many years after the death of his predecessor in the same role?
Renowned Abstract Expressionist painter Clyfford Still graduated from college in 1933. As of August 1, 2024, what other alumnus from that university was nominated for a Pulitzer Prize?
What is the the sum of the ages of the men who were executed by firing squad in Kilmainham Gaol, on the 12th of May, during the same year as the Battle of the Somme, when the died?
When the maker of the third-party console title "ActRaiser", merged with the makers of the console title "Chrono Trigger", what percentage of the company did the makers of "ActRaiser" make up?

How many years apart were the start of the Haitian and French revolutions , how many years longer did the Haitian Revolution last than the French Revolution, and how old were their respective leaders when each revolution began?
Are mulberries more related to raspberries or cannabis?

There is a speech-sound disorder, not caused by a structural abnormality, for which one symptom is rearranging sounds of a word. The term for the disorder was first defined in 1908 by a German neuroscientist with the first name Hugo. Why was the Nobel Peace Prize given to an American the same year Hugo died?
How old was Russian vodka tycoon Yuri Shefler when Serene, the yacht he commissioned, was delivered to him?
Who won the World Series the same year that Andy Roddick won his only Grand Slam title in 2003?
What was the name of the work written by Louis Pierre Vieillot published two years after he described the great blue turao as Musophaga cristata?
What military awards were received by the General originally scheduled to lead Operation Torch before Lieutenant General Dwight D. Eisenhower was given command of the operation?

From United States Former President Bill Clinton through Former President Donald Trump, which president in this time period has published the greatest number of books as of August 1, 2024? Please exclude from that count any books you find that are authored by someone else about a particular former president.
What US president was born in the same year that the Treaty of Resht was signed?
Which horse won the Kentucky Derby during the same calendar year in which John Hinckley Jr. attempted to assassinate U.S. President Ronald Reagan?

Milton Friedman won the Nobel Prize for Economics in 1976. What was the name of the Nobel Peace Prize winning wife of the economist who won the Nobel Prize for Economics two years before Friedman did?
When Justin Trudeau was elected as Prime Minister of Canada, who was the current Prime Minister of France?

This town was the original location of the tallest Christmas Tree displayed at the Rockefeller Center. This was also home to the author of a famous children's book series. What is the name of this series?

Houston, Texas had dozens of airports as of January 1, 2024. Find the three-letter IATA code of the airport in which the longest runway was exactly 7000 feet long, and rearrange those letters to match that of another airport at that time.  Here are your hints: The last letter of the re-arranged code is "J". The new airport was located in China.   With this information, what was the IATA code of this airport?
How many years earlier did Wimbledon start compared to the birthdate of the winner of the 2019 tournament.
The starship USS Enterprise has a registry number containing the founding year of what Ivy League university?
What two cities hosted the Summer Olympic Games between when the television shows featuring characters Olivia Benson and Meredith Grey started airing?
Who had their twenty-first number one hit on the US Billboard Hot Country Songs chart the same week Carly Rae Jepsen hit #39 on the Australia ARIA Top 50 Singles chart?
What was the word that featured the least in the longest recorded quotation by the chimpanzee named after the recipient of the 2011 Sydney Peace Prize?
What song was #1 on Billboard's Hot 100 for the most days during the Cuban Missile Crisis?
Which ancient archaeoastronomical site in the U.K. is also associated with the summer solstice during which time light illuminates a quartz-rich stone in the chamber.
What is the price difference of an iPhone (8GB) from when it was first released compared to the price of the iPhone X when it was released?
What's the star sign of the author of A Court of Thorns and Roses?
What was the average launch mass of Apollo 11, Apollo 12, and Apollo 13 in kilograms, rounded to the nearest integer?
What was the second starring role of the actress who won an Oscar for portraying union activist Cyrstal Lee Sutton?

What is the scientific name of an amphibian that is listed as endangered by the Canadian government (as of 2024), and its only population in Canada occurs on an island which is the southernmost inhabited part of Canada?
What were the names of the parents of the first overall pick in the 2007 NHL entry draft?
Of the counties that Wisconsin Highway 34 runs through, what is the seat of the most populous county based on 2020 census data?
Emma Lazarus's most famous poem inspired the founding of an order of nursing nuns.  What disease does this order specialize in treating?
How many times did the victor of the Immortal Game of 1851 lose a chess match to an American-born opponent?

How many years were between the publication of a book considered 'one of the seminal works of fiction of the 20th century', and the Japanese release of the retail version of the game "Resident Evil: Revelations 2" for the PS3, of which the author of the 20th century book was a great inspiration for the plot?

The actress who played Aunt Rose in A Brooklyn State of Mind (1997) also starred in a mafia movie where she sang a popular Sicilian song. How many years later did her version of the song occur after the earliest recording?
Put in chronological order the Major League Baseball seasons in which Barry Bonds, Tony Gwynn, and Benny Kauff hit for a .370 batting average.
What famous film maker once provided editorial assistance for a 90s documentary on Mongolian-Tuvan throat singing before directing a series of superhero movies?
How many of the first 8 Harry Potter films based on the original 7 books were released in years when a United States presidential election took place, and what movies were they?
In which year did the 4th Sheriff of Yorkshire to be part of the House of Plantagenet die?
How many more medals did France win in the 2008 Summer Olympics than in the 2004 Summer Olympics?
How many colours are on the flag of the country whose capital is the southernmost by latitude out of all landlocked countries, as of 2024? What are the names of these colours?

This 90s rock musical depiction of La bohème, had this music, lyrics and story written by a talent who passed the day before their Off-Broadway preview performance. Aside from Broadway, what was the name of his Netflix success?
What German-born Author had books published in 1995, 1999, & 2005 detailing their studies and activism with what Tang's animal mascot of the time?
This author won the Popular Fiction Book of the Year award in 2009 at the Irish Book Awards. What is their astrological sign?
What is the name of the 2nd track on the album by Corinne Bailey Rae that came out 10 years before her album The Heart Speaks in Whispers?

Which of the following albums came out on a date closest to the date that Nancy Kerrigan was assaulted? What about which album was closest to the date of Harding's plea deal? Awake by Dream Theater Inside Out by Fates Warning Promised Land by Queensryche Dreamspace by Stratovarius
Which U.S. National Park was the first to be established after the Portland Trail Blazers won their only playoff series with LaMarcus Aldridge on the team?
